comment_text: I am a Wednesday's child. I never knew the significance of it or thought much about it until I watched Wednesday on Netflix, particularly the first episode. If you remember the scene with Principal Weems at Nevermore, she tells Wednesday her name is unique and asks her if she was born on a Wednesday. Morticia says she was named after a line in her favorite nursery rhyme, "Wednesday's child is full of woe" (Unfortunately true). I didn't realize the new trailer for Season 2 had recently come out and I just found this a little while ago. I've been waiting forever for Season 2. And what's more, today, June 3rd, is my birthday. I absolutely love Wednesday and feel connected to her somehow. Thank you Netflix for a great birthday gift for this Wednesday's child.

video_title: Wednesday: Season 2 | Official Teaser Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
This looks so good! Love how they seem to be leaning more into the darker side of things, it also looks like some of the side characters will be getting more screentime which is nice.

Also can we talk about Enid's updated look? I love how this new look really makes her look like she's become more confident in herself since wolfing-out and standing up to her mother last season.

Like when you look at her fashion sense from last season for instance, you'll notice that even though it's colourful, it's kind of muted. She wears a lot of colour yes...But it's pastels, and majority of her clothes are quite baggy.

But then here in this scene everything is a lot brighter, her hair visibly stands out, she has bright eyeshadow, and even her uniform seems to be a slightly brighter shade of blue. Hell, I wouldn't be shocked if we see her nails in the first episode and they've gone from those pastels to much brighters versions of themselves. And her uniform itself is much more form fitting than it was before, plus the bowtie instead of the regular tie makes it look a lot more cutesy, which is very fitting for Enid.

Her hair too is quite different. Instead of having pastel dyed ends with a slight wave in it, it's straight with much brighter dyed segments that extend to roughly the midlength of her hair.

Plus the ripped leggings and the swap from wavy hair to straight, to me at least it kind of gives the impression that she doesn't feel the need to impress anyone, like she's okay with imperfection.

All of the new additions and changes in her outfit give the impression of someone who is comfortable with who they are and doesn't feel the need to change themselves to impress others.
